revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:йқ’йә»зҘһзӨҫ]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Aoso Shrine | image = File:Aoso jinja Haiden.JPG | coordinates = {{Coord|38.335325|N|140.935094|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Aosozawa, Iwakiri, Miyagino-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§еҫЎзҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|жңҲиӘӯзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғ„гӮҜгғЁгғҹ}} * {{ill|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|ja|еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|1=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|2=ja|3=еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|4=pl|5=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HitachibЕҚ Kaison|pl|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481691}} (enshrined deity) | founded = {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|qid=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 2 (852 CE) | festivals = May 1 | former status = {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | prefecture= {{ill|Miyagino-ku|qid=Q1043355|1=Miyagino-ku, Sendai}} }} '''Aoso Shrine''' ({{nihongo|йқ’йә»зҘһзӨҫ|гҒӮгҒҠгҒқгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғ|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Miyagino-ku, Sendai|en|Miyagino-ku, Sendai|qid=Q1043355|lt=Miyagino-ku|1=Miyagino-ku, Sendai}} district of {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}}, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is the head shrine of all Aoso Shrines and Sanko Shrines in Japan. Its historical status under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (heavenly god of the stars) * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§еӨ§еҫЎзҘһ|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} (sun goddess) * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|жңҲиӘӯзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғ„гӮҜгғЁгғҹ}} (moon deity) These three deities represent celestial forces: the stars, the sun, and the moon. During the era of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ|en|Shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ|1=Shinbutsu-shЕ«gЕҚ}}, they were equated with {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}, {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=FudЕҚ MyЕҚЕҚ|1=Acala}}, and {{ill|ДҖkДҒЕӣagarbha|en|ДҖkДҒЕӣagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=KokЕ«zЕҚ Bosatsu|1=ДҖkДҒЕӣagarbha}}, respectively. Additionally, {{ill|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|ja|еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|1=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|2=ja|3=еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|4=pl|5=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HitachibЕҚ Kaison|pl|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481691}}, famed for curing strokes, is also enshrined. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was founded in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|qid=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 2 (852 CE) when Hozumi Tamotsu, an ancestor of the shrine's priestly family, settled in the region from {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tamotsu enshrined the three celestial deities in a cave, establishing the shrine. The name "Aoso" (йқ’йә», "green hemp") and the shrine's crest, a hemp leaf design, are attributed to Tamotsu teaching the locals how to cultivate hemp. In {{ill|Tenna|ja|еӨ©е’Ң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442381}} 2 (1682), {{ill|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|ja|еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|1=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|2=ja|3=еёёйҷёеқҠжө·е°Ҡ|4=pl|5=HitachibЕҚ Kaison|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HitachibЕҚ Kaison|pl|HitachibЕҚ Kaison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481691}}, said to be a retainer of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qid=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}, was enshrined following accounts of his healing miracles. The shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in May 1875 during the Meiji era. == External links == * [http://www12.plala.or.jp/aosojin/ Official website] (in Japanese) {{Commonscat|Aoso-jinja|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|йқ’йә»зҘһзӨҫ|version=104612602|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:йқ’йә»зҘһзӨҫ]]
